reason: The statement applies because Joshua, the son of Nun, was chosen by Yahweh to be a prophet. In the provided text fragment, Joshua is described as one of the men sent to spy out the land of Canaan. He demonstrated faith and courage in believing that the Israelites could possess the land, and he was favored by Yahweh for his loyalty and trust. Furthermore, Joshua's role as a prophet is further emphasized in later biblical texts.
